title: MCP Resources primitive + notifications: AGENTS.md resource + tools/list_changed (Rec#12 + Rec#14)
description: Extend the MCP server with the Resource and Notification primitives from MCP 2025-06-18: (1) register AGENTS.md as a subscribable MCP resource so agents can cache L0 rules client-side and receive notifications/resources/updated after sync-meta, (2) send notifications/tools/list_changed to all active sessions when sync-meta detects agent registry changes. Together these let agents proactively refresh their context without polling.

changes: Register AGENTS.md as MCP resource: { uri: 'fabric://agents-md', name: 'AGENTS.md', mimeType: 'text/markdown', subscribe: true }
Add resource read handler that returns current AGENTS.md content
Add notifications/resources/updated dispatch after sync-meta completes (wire into chokidar watcher or update_registry write path)
Add notifications/tools/list_changed dispatch when agents.meta.json changes (agent registry mutation detected)
Expose active sessions Map to notification dispatch functions
Add notifyAllSessions(notification) helper that iterates sessions Map and sends MCP notification to each
Wire chokidar watcher meta-change event → notifyAllSessions(tools/list_changed) + notifyAllSessions(resources/updated)
Reuse TASK-001 ContextCache watcher callbacks as the trigger point (no second watcher needed)
When MCP resource is registered: L0 field in fab_get_rules response becomes resource URI reference ('fabric://agents-md') instead of inlined full text
Retain full-text fallback for clients that don't support resources subscription

implementation: Read packages/server/src/index.ts to understand current McpServer setup and existing tool registration pattern
Read MCP SDK documentation for server.resource() registration API and notifications/resources/updated dispatch
Read http.ts sessions Map structure to understand how to iterate and send notifications to all active clients
Implement MCP resource registration for AGENTS.md in index.ts (read handler uses ContextCache from TASK-001)
Implement notifyAllSessions helper in http.ts iterating sessions Map
Wire chokidar watcher (from TASK-001 ContextCache) to trigger: notifyAllSessions('notifications/resources/updated') + notifyAllSessions('notifications/tools/list_changed')
Modify get-rules.ts: detect if calling client has subscribed to fabric://agents-md resource; if so, return URI ref instead of full L0 text
Test: subscribe to resource, trigger sync-meta, confirm notifications received by connected MCP client

reference.pattern: MCP SDK server.resource() registration and notification dispatch
reference.examples: Mirror the tool registration pattern for resource registration; notifications/resources/updated follows the same session-broadcast pattern as existing MCP request handlers

rationale.chosen_approach: Register AGENTS.md resource + both notification types together to share the same notification dispatch infrastructure
alternatives_considered: Separate tasks for resource (Rec#12) and notifications (Rec#14) — they share notifyAllSessions infrastructure, no benefit in splitting
Polling-based resource refresh (defeats the purpose of subscribe:true)
decision_factors: TASK-001 ContextCache provides the file-watch trigger needed for resource/tools notifications — dependency is justified
Both notification types share the same session-iteration pattern in http.ts
L0 resource URI change in get-rules.ts reduces payload size for subscribed agents — natural co-location
rationale.tradeoffs: Depends on TASK-001 completing first; L0 URI reference change is a behavior change for subscribed clients (non-breaking for non-subscribers)

risks.description: MCP SDK resource subscription API may differ from expected API surface in this project's SDK version
risks.mitigation: Check @modelcontextprotocol/sdk version and its resource/notification API; prototype resource registration before full implementation
risks.fallback: Implement resource read handler without subscribe flag if subscription is unsupported; add notifications independently

risks.description: Sending notifications to all sessions on every meta change may be noisy if meta updates frequently
risks.mitigation: Debounce notifications with 200ms window; only notify on structural changes (node add/remove), not data updates
risks.fallback: Gate notifications behind a FABRIC_MCP_NOTIFICATIONS=true env flag

risks.description: L0 URI reference change in get-rules response may break agents that expect inline L0 content
risks.mitigation: Only return URI reference for clients that have explicitly subscribed to the resource; non-subscribed clients always get inline L0
risks.fallback: Keep inline L0 by default, make URI-reference opt-in via client capability negotiation

criteria: MCP server resources/list response includes fabric://agents-md resource entry
Subscribing to fabric://agents-md and triggering sync-meta causes notifications/resources/updated to arrive at subscribed client
After agent registry change (update_registry call), all active MCP sessions receive notifications/tools/list_changed
An agent subscribed to fabric://agents-md receives L0 as resource URI reference in fab_get_rules response
Non-subscribed agents still receive inline L0 content in fab_get_rules (backward compatibility)
convergence.verification: Use MCP inspector: subscribe to fabric://agents-md; run fab sync-meta; confirm notifications/resources/updated received; run fab_update_registry; confirm notifications/tools/list_changed received
convergence.definition_of_done: MCP-connected agents can subscribe to AGENTS.md and receive push notifications on any fabric state change, eliminating need for periodic fab_get_rules polling

unit: notifyAllSessions: iterates all sessions Map entries and calls server.notification() on each
Resource read handler: returns current AGENTS.md content from ContextCache
get-rules: returns L0 as URI reference when client is subscribed, inline text otherwise
integration: Connect MCP client, subscribe to fabric://agents-md, trigger meta change, confirm notifications/resources/updated
Connect MCP client, trigger update_registry, confirm notifications/tools/list_changed
manual_checks: MCP inspector: resources/read for fabric://agents-md returns AGENTS.md content
After fab sync-meta: confirm notification received in MCP inspector session
success_metrics: Subscribed agents receive push notifications within 500ms of meta change
Backward compatibility: unsubscribed clients receive identical responses to pre-implementation baseline

commit.message_template: feat(server): AGENTS.md MCP Resource (subscribe) + notifications/resources/updated + notifications/tools/list_changed
